articles.title: Theoretical Distinctions Between Emotion and Cognition Content in Supportive Messages: Examining Effects and Mediating Mechanisms
articles.abstract: Communication Research, Ahead of Print. What constitutes effective supportive message content is a topic of intense interdisciplinary scholarly interest and has palpable real-world implications for individuals’ well-being. Emotion and cognition content are two commonly theorized supportive ...
articles.authors: Amanda J. Holmstrom, Junwen M. Hu, Samantha J. Shebib, Jong In Lim, Yue Zhang, Andrew Bredland, Allison P. Mazur, Adam J. Mason, Amanda Allard, Josephine K. Boumis, Hailey Schulte, Ji Hyun Ha, Brooke
themes: Supportive message content theory: distinction between emotion-focused and cognition-focused support
Mediating mechanisms underlying supportive communication effectiveness
Psychological well-being outcomes of differentiated message content
articles.core_question: How do emotion content and cognition content in supportive messages differentially affect well-being outcomes, and what mediating mechanisms explain these effects?

articles.title: Gains, Limits, and Side-Effects of Disinformation Interventions: Corrective Information and Self-Affirmation Assessed With Signal Detection Theory
articles.abstract: Communication Research, Ahead of Print. articles.core_question: How do corrective information and self-affirmation interventions affect the accuracy with which individuals discriminate between true and false political claims, and what are their potential side-effects beyond accuracy gains?

articles.core_question: How do mom creators on Xiaohongshu articulate maternal pain and reproductive suffering as a form of digital victimhood, and what does this platform-mediated mode of maternal subjectivity reveal about the intersection of gendered experiences and the creator economy?

articles.title: Are smartphones stress-inducing or stress-buffering for adolescents? An experience-sampling study
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. Despite the far-reaching impact of stress on overall well-being, current research offers little information on whether smartphone use (SU) is stress-inducing or stress-buffering for adolescents. Building on existing media effects theories and the ...
articles.authors: Michaela Šaradín Lebedíková, David Lacko, Ine Beyens, David Smahel
themes: Media effects and psychological well-being: The theoretical tension between smartphones as stressors versus buffers, drawing on media effects theories to explain dual pathways of technology influence on adolescent mental health
Mobile communication and adolescent development: The role of smartphone use in shaping daily affective experiences and coping mechanisms during a formative developmental period
Ecological momentary assessment of digital media use: Applying intensive longitudinal methods to capture real-time, within-person relationships between smartphone behavior and psychological states
articles.core_question: Are smartphones stress-inducing or stress-buffering for adolescents when examined through the lens of daily, within-person experiences?

articles.title: Balancing moral, financial, and legal considerations: How practitioners in the game industry justify spending on accessibility
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. As the global game industry expands, accessibility for players with disabilities has become a growing concern. Implementing accessibility is mostly technologically feasible, yet it remains scarce. This study investigates how practitioners justify ...
articles.authors: Lobna Hassan, Miikka J. Lehtonen, Mohammed (Abdelaty) Mohammed, Karen Schrier
themes: Institutional logics and moral economy in accessibility work
Disability studies and social inclusion in digital media industries
Organizational justification and decision-making under competing institutional pressures
articles.core_question: How do game industry practitioners discursively justify spending on accessibility by navigating tensions between moral imperatives, financial constraints, and legal compliance?

articles.title: “He will always love me”: Authentic romance and reciprocal love in otome games
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. This study investigates how players construct and articulate emotionally meaningful relationships in otome games. Drawing from 293 open-ended responses from Chinese players, a thematic analysis identified six recurring themes:authentic romance,...
articles.authors: Elena Yifei Zhao, Nicholas D Bowman
themes: Parasocial intimacy and affective bonds in human-AI/character interactions
Authenticity construction in mediated romantic relationships
Gender, agency, and emotional labor in digital gameplay cultures
articles.core_question: How do players of otome games construct and articulate the meaning of emotionally authentic romantic relationships within a procedurally constrained digital narrative environment?

articles.title: From search engines to AI agents: Interface control and the restructuring of communication power
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. Control over digital interfaces has become a significant aspect of geopolitical struggles. This article advances an analytical framework illuminating how global communication power manifests across three key interfaces: search engines, social media, and ...
themes: Interface control as a site of geopolitical power in digital communication
Restructuring of communication power through algorithmic governance
Critical political economy of platform interfaces (search engines, social media, AI agents)
articles.core_question: How does control over digital interfaces (search engines, social media, AI agents) function as a form of global communication power and restructure geopolitical struggles?

articles.title: “Douyin is taming us”: Visibility, governance, and feminist discourses on Douyin
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. Drawing on 14 in-depth interviews, this study examines how feminist content creators on Douyin navigate platform visibility, governance, and gendered censorship to disseminate feminist discourse. Findings show that participants position themselves as...
themes: Platform visibility governance and algorithmic control
Feminist discourse navigation of gendered censorship
Digital labor and self-regulation of content creators

articles.title: From For You to the family table: Adolescent consumer learning and reverse socialization on TikTok
articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. This study examines how TikTok functions as an agent of consumer socialization and facilitates intergenerational knowledge exchange within families. Drawing on 20 in-depth interviews with adolescents aged 11–16, the study investigates how they acquire, ...

Reverse intergenerational influence
Social media as socialization agent

articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. This study extends uncertainty reduction theory beyond dyadic interaction by introducingcommunal uncertainty reduction strategiesin human-AI socio-emotional communication, wherein users navigate AI-related uncertainty by engaging with both AI chatbots ...
articles.authors: Hongyuan Gan, Han Li, Jinyuan Zhan, Renwen Zhang

Human-AI socio-emotional communication and relational dynamics
Communal coping strategies for technology-related ambiguity

articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. While previous research has established foundational knowledge on how women in STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math) social media content creators use digital platforms to combat stereotypes, less is known about how they make space for ...
articles.authors: Jiyoun Suk, Sara Holland Levin, Jocelyn Steinke, Kyle Schnitzer, Amanda Coletti, Christine Gilbert, Anne Oeldorf-Hirsch

Visual space-making and platform affordances for marginalized groups

articles.abstract: New Media & Society, Ahead of Print. This mixed-methods study explores how children aged 5–12 years experience and respond to perceived misinformation on social media. articles.authors: Freja Sørine Adler Berg, Lene Heiselberg, Thomas Enemark Lundtofte, Lena Frischlich

Digital media socialization and platform-based information veracity

articles.title: Mitigating the News-Finds-Me Perception: Evaluating the intended (and unintended) effects of educational warnings on political learning

Intervention design and unintended consequences in media literacy education

# 本周新闻传播学研究前沿风向

**分析时间**: 2026/5/1
**文章总数**: 45

## 一、热点主题排行

1. Supportive message content theory: distinction between emotion-focused and cognition-focused support (出现 1 次)
2. Mediating mechanisms underlying supportive communication effectiveness (出现 1 次)

4. Misinformation correction and its boundary conditions (出现 1 次)
5. Signal Detection Theory as a framework for assessing information processing accuracy (出现 1 次)
6. Psychological resistance and persuasion (self-affirmation theory) in political communication (出现 1 次)
7. Context-dependency of media effects across issue domains (war/conflict vs. immigration) (出现 1 次)
8. Hybrid organizing and institutional logics in organizations pursuing dual/multiple goals (出现 1 次)
9. Resilience enactment as a communicative and symbolic practice in organizational contexts (出现 1 次)
10. Representational networks and social media as sites for constructing organizational identity and legitimacy (出现 1 次)

本周期研究明显向"Supportive message content theory: distinction between emotion-focused and cognition-focused support"方向倾斜，Supportive message content theory: distinction between emotion-focused and cognition-focused support成为核心驱动变量。
